what_it_does: Combines two governance systems in one file. (1) Export surfaces: tokens for print/PDF page sizes (A4 portrait/landscape, Letter) and slide frames (16x9, 4x3) plus a page margin, a .page class for on-screen authoring of those frames, landscape/slide modifiers, and a @media print block that paints backgrounds, hides chrome, and forces one page per .page. (2) Lifecycle tags: a .lc badge with experimental/stable/deprecated variants coloured from zone roles, plus data-lifecycle whole-object hints (dimmed deprecated, dashed-outline experimental).
observations: Page frame tokens given in CSS px @96dpi with explicit dimensions (A4 794x1123, landscape 1123x794, Letter 816x1056, slide 16x9 1920x1080, 4x3 1440x1080)
.page defaults to A4 portrait via --page-w/--page-h fallbacks, centers with margin-inline:auto, uses --zone-ground bg and --elev-3 shadow
.page.slide drops padding to 0; .page.landscape swaps to landscape dimensions
@media print sets --zone-intensity:1, white bg, hides .no-print/[data-no-print], removes page shadow/margin, break-after:page, and forces print-color-adjust:exact
.lc badge uses font-mono, uppercase, a leading currentColor dot; variants pull color/background/border from zone-warning/success/reject role tokens
.lc-deprecated adds line-through; [data-lifecycle=deprecated] sets opacity 0.6, experimental adds a dashed warning outline
standouts: Treats deliverables (print/PDF/slides) as first-class surfaces that compose against a known page just as screens compose against a viewport - export-native alongside mobile-native
Lifecycle tags visibly quarantine experimental/deprecated work so it cannot silently rot, with both a badge and a whole-object visual hint
conventions: Surface-as-target framing consistent with the system's axis/surface model
Lifecycle colours reuse zone role tokens (warning/success/reject) rather than new colours
Caller-overridable frame via --page-w/--page-h with sensible fallbacks (knob pattern)
notes: Two governance concerns bundled: export page/slide frame tokens + a print-ready .page surface, and lifecycle (experimental/stable/deprecated) badges plus whole-object hints, all coloured from zone roles.
questions: Why are export surfaces and lifecycle tags co-located in one file rather than split - is this an intentional grouping or convenience?
Where are --zone-ground, --elev-3, --zone-intensity, and the zone role tokens (warning/success/reject ink/surface/edge) defined?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the header declares two governance systems (export surfaces and lifecycle tags) and the file implements both; the only nuance is that two distinct concerns share one file.
apparent_themes: deliverables as surfaces (export-native parity with screen/mobile)
visible quarantine of experimental/deprecated work (anti-rot governance)
reuse of zone role colours for new semantic states
print fidelity (exact colour, page breaks, chrome removal)
intention_signals: Aims to make handoff packages, PDFs, and decks compose against known page/slide frames the same way UIs compose against viewports, and to give the design system a lifecycle governance vocabulary so consumers can see at a glance what is safe, experimental, or deprecated.
novelty: Framing print pages and slide frames as first-class compositional surfaces, and pairing that with a lifecycle-tagging system that both badges and visually marks the whole object.
contribution: Contributes the export-surface vocabulary (page/slide frame tokens, the .page authoring surface, the print stylesheet) and the lifecycle governance vocabulary (the .lc badge variants and data-lifecycle object hints).
summary_for_embedding: A CSS file defining export-surface tokens (A4/Letter/slide page frames), a printable .page authoring surface with a print media block, and lifecycle tags (.lc experimental/stable/deprecated badges plus data-lifecycle whole-object hints) coloured from zone roles.
intention_for_embedding: To make deliverables (print, PDF, slides) first-class surfaces that compose against known pages, and to add a lifecycle governance vocabulary that visibly quarantines experimental and deprecated components so consumers know what is safe.
